blocks.title: Patient {{ patient_id }} — {{ classify.document_class }}
blocks.content:
Classification confidence: **{{ classify.confidence }}**. Reasoning: _{{ classify.reasoning }}_.

Citations failed for **{{ verify.unverified_count }}** of {{ persist.field_count }} extracted field(s).

blocks.title: Unverified field keys
blocks.content:
**Flagged:** {{ verify.unverified_keys }}

Verification compares each citation's `supporting_text` against the OCR output (lowercased + whitespace collapsed). A field is flagged when its citation is **not a substring** of what kreuzberg actually read.

Common causes:

- The LLM **silently corrected** an OCR glitch in its citation — e.g. it cites `0.6 - 1.1` when the OCR actually read `@.6- 1.1` for that reference range.
- The LLM **rephrased** the source span instead of quoting it verbatim.
- The OCR genuinely doesn't contain the cited value (LLM hallucination).

Compare the citation in the **OCR span** column above against the document image. If the value is correct on the page, approve; if the LLM invented it, reject.

field.name: edits
field.label: Edited fields (JSON, only if Decision = Approve with edits)
field.placeholder: Paste a JSON object overriding any of the extracted field values, e.g. {"Haemoglobin": "14.2 g/dL"}. Leave blank to accept the extraction as-is.

field.name: note
field.label: Reviewer note (optional)
field.placeholder: Anything the next reviewer should know.
